title: Roasted Vietnamese Corn Banh Cuon
description: A flexitarian-friendly Vietnamese dish featuring roasted corn arepa filled with mozzarella cheese, served in a banh cuon form. This dish is a creative twist on traditional Vietnamese cuisine.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, mix together the corn kernels, mozzarella cheese, and arepa cornmeal.
Gradually add in the water, stirring until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes until it becomes pliable.
Divide the dough into 4 equal pieces and roll each piece into a ball.
Flatten each ball into a thin circle, about 1/8 inch thick.
Heat the vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
Cook the corn arepa for 2-3 minutes on each side, until lightly browned.
Transfer the arepa to a baking sheet and roast in the oven for 10-12 minutes, until crispy.
While the arepa is roasting, heat the nimono simmer sauce in a small saucepan over low heat.
To assemble the banh cuon, place a roasted arepa on a flat surface and spoon some of the nimono simmer sauce down the center.
Roll the arepa into a tight cylinder and slice in half.

axes.primaryIngredient: corn arepa filled with mozarella cheese
